perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement (including information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Proprietary Data disclosed under this Agreement (including information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' in Section 5 creates an inconsistency because the document defines 'Confidential Information' in Section 1. This creates an ambiguity as to what the Receiving Party is obligated to return or destroy, as it now refers to 'Proprietary Data' which is not explicitly defined, potentially contradicting the obligations defined around 'Confidential Information.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "Confidential Information" means information in whatever form disclosed by or on behalf of one Party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other Party (the "Receiving Party") before, on or after the Effective Date hereof which relates to a Disclosing Party's business or the Transaction including without limitation: business, financial, human resources, and technical materials, information and data, or information which although not directly related to the Transaction, is nevertheless disclosed as a result of or in connection with the Parties' discussions of the Transaction together with analyses or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or any of the Receiving Party's affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants that contain or otherwise reflect such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' which is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text.' The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Data,' which is not defined in the document. This creates a contradiction because the receiving party's obligations regarding the handling and return of information are now ambiguous, as they are tied to an undefined term instead of the clearly defined 'Confidential Information,' potentially creating inconsistency. This directly adheres to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date hereof, neither it nor any of its affiliates will solicit for employment, directly or indirectly, any member of the other Party's senior management with whom it first had contact, or who was specifically identified to it, during the period of its evaluation of the Transaction; provided, however, that this paragraph will not prevent either Party from -------- ------- employing any person who contacts such Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date hereof, neither it nor any of its affiliates will solicit for employment, directly or indirectly, any member of the other Party's top executives with whom it first had contact, or who was specifically identified to it, during the period of its evaluation of the Transaction; provided, however, that this paragraph will not prevent either Party from -------- ------- employing any person who contacts such Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'senior management' to 'top executives' introduces ambiguity. While seemingly similar, the specific roles included in 'senior management' might be broader or narrower than 'top executives.' This can lead to disputes regarding which employees are covered by the non-solicitation clause. The use of 'top executives' is not defined, creating potential loopholes or disagreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date hereof, neither it nor any of its affiliates will solicit for employment, directly or indirectly, any member of the other Party's top executives with whom it first had contact, or who was specifically identified to it, during the period of its evaluation of the Transaction; provided, however, that this paragraph will not prevent either Party from -------- ------- employing any person who contacts such Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such Party. For purposes of this paragraph, "solicit for employment" shall not be deemed to include any general solicitations of employment by one Party not specifically directed towards employees of the other Party. Nothing herein shall prevent either Party from, directly or indirectly, soliciting for employment or hiring any member of the other Party's senior management whose employment with such other Party has been terminated.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'senior management' to 'top executives.' The contradicted text uses both 'top executives' and 'senior management,' and the explanation states this change can create ambiguity because 'top executives' is not defined. Since the contradicted text uses both terms, but the changed text uses only 'top executives,' which may have a different meaning than what was previously agreed, it creates a contradiction because the scope of the non-solicitation clause is now unclear, fitting the definition of Misaligned Terminology causing an In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and for the purposes of the ongoing Transaction, and shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to third parties, using the same degree of care used to protect its own proprietary information of like importance, but in any case using no less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall use the Disclosing Party's Private Details only for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction and for the purposes of the ongoing Transaction, and shall protect such Private Details from disclosure to third parties, using the same degree of care used to protect its own proprietary information of like importance, but in any case using no less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Details' creates a contradiction. Section 1 defines 'Confidential Information,' but 'Private Details' is not defined. This leaves uncertainty as to what specific data the Receiving Party is obligated to protect, conflicting with the defined scope of 'Confidential Information.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "Confidential Information" means information in whatever form disclosed by or on behalf of one Party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other Party (the "Receiving Party") before, on or after the Effective Date hereof which relates to a Disclosing Party's business or the Transaction including without limitation: business, financial, human resources, and technical materials, information and data, or information which although not directly related to the Transaction, is nevertheless disclosed as a result of or in connection with the Parties' discussions of the Transaction together with analyses or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or any of the Receiving Party's affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants that contain or otherwise reflect such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Details.' The contradicted text provides a specific definition for 'Confidential Information'. Because 'Private Details' is undefined, this creates a contradiction as it introduces ambiguity regarding what information the Receiving Party is obligated to protect, thus conflicting with the defined scope outlined in the 'Confidential Information' definition. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology which leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them.

perturbation.original_text: Without the prior written consent of the other Party, a Party will not disclose (other than as anticipated elsewhere in this Agreement) to any third party any information (including Confidential Information) regarding the Transaction, including without limitation, the fact that discussions are occurring concerning the Transaction, any of the terms or conditions relating to the Transaction being discussed by the Parties, or the existence of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Without the prior written consent of the other Party, a Party will not disclose (other than as anticipated elsewhere in this Agreement) to any third party any data (including Confidential Information) regarding the Transaction, including without limitation, the fact that discussions are occurring concerning the Transaction, any of the terms or conditions relating to the Transaction being discussed by the Parties, or the existence of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'data', when the original text said 'including Confidential Information', now it is using a different term, data, that is not defined or specified and, if you consider it as the broadest term, it expands the scope of what cannot be disclosed beyond 'Confidential Information', leading to uncertainty. What is considered data? Anything in any form? Which goes in contradiction with Confidential Information as defined in the document
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without the prior written consent of the other Party, a Party will not disclose (other than as anticipated elsewhere in this Agreement) to any third party any data (including Confidential Information) regarding the Transaction, including without limitation, the fact that discussions are occurring concerning the Transaction, any of the terms or conditions relating to the Transaction being discussed by the Parties, or the existence of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'information (including Confidential Information)'. The changed text substitutes 'information' with 'data'. This introduces a contradiction because 'data' is a broader, undefined term that potentially encompasses more than just 'Confidential Information' as defined elsewhere in the contract, leading to uncertainty about the scope of non-disclosure obligations. This contradicts the intention of only restricting the disclosure of Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: Dimension hereby acknowledges that Proxicom is disclosing its Confidential Information in consideration of Dimension's agreement not to propose to Proxicom or any other person or entity any transaction between Dimension and Proxicom and/or its security holders or involving any of Proxicom's securities or security holders unless Proxicom shall have requested in writing that Dimension make such a proposal, and that Dimension will not acquire, or assist, advise or encourage any other persons or entities in acquiring, directly or indirectly, control of Proxicom or any of Proxicom's securities, businesses or assets for a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement unless Proxicom shall have consented in advance in writing to such acquisition.
perturbation.changed_text: Dimension hereby acknowledges that Proxicom is disclosing its sensitive data in consideration of Dimension's agreement not to propose to Proxicom or any other person or entity any transaction between Dimension and Proxicom and/or its security holders or involving any of Proxicom's securities or security holders unless Proxicom shall have requested in writing that Dimension make such a proposal, and that Dimension will not acquire, or assist, advise or encourage any other persons or entities in acquiring, directly or indirectly, control of Proxicom or any of Proxicom's securities, businesses or assets for a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement unless Proxicom shall have consented in advance in writing to such acquisition.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'sensitive data' creates an in-text contradiction and confusion. 'Confidential Information' is a defined term in section 1 of the agreement. 'Sensitive data' is undefined, thus broadening the scope of information subject to the non-compete and non-solicitation restrictions in this section. The contradiction arises between the defined scope of protection in Section 1, and the potentially much broader, undefined scope implied by "sensitive data" in Section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "Confidential Information" means information in whatever form disclosed by or on behalf of one Party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other Party (the "Receiving Party") before, on or after the Effective Date hereof which relates to a Disclosing Party's business or the Transaction including without limitation: business, financial, human resources, and technical materials, information and data, or information which although not directly related to the Transaction, is nevertheless disclosed as a result of or in connection with the Parties' discussions of the Transaction together with analyses or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or any of the Receiving Party's affiliates, employees, representatives and/or consultants that contain or otherwise reflect such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' which is a defined term in Section 1. The changed text replaces this with 'sensitive data,' which is not defined. This creates a contradiction because Section 1 provides a specific scope for 'Confidential Information,' while 'sensitive data' introduces an undefined and potentially broader scope, leading to uncertainty about what information is protected under the agreement, directly conflicting with the defined term in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party acknowledges that it is aware (and that its affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants who are apprised of the possible Transaction have been advised) that the United States and other applicable securities laws prohibit any person who is aware of material, non-public information about a company obtained directly or indirectly from that company, from purchasing or selling securities of such company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party acknowledges that it is aware (and that its affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants who are apprised of the possible Transaction have been advised) that the United States and other applicable securities laws prohibit any person who is aware of material, non-public data about a company obtained directly or indirectly from that company, from purchasing or selling securities of such company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'information' with 'data' regarding securities laws creates an in-text contradiction. Securities laws typically refer to 'material non-public information'. By substituting 'data,' it broadens the scope, potentially including information that is not traditionally considered 'information' under securities law, creating uncertainty and potential conflicts in interpretation. What is now considered data? Code? Infrastructure information? It goes against material non-public information
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges that it is aware (and that its affiliates, employees, representatives and consultants who are apprised of the possible Transaction have been advised) that the United States and other applicable securities laws prohibit any person who is aware of material, non-public data about a company obtained directly or indirectly from that company, from purchasing or selling securities of such company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'information' to 'data' broadens the scope of what is prohibited under securities laws. The contradicted text uses the term 'data' while securities laws typically focus on 'material non-public information.' This creates a strong in-text contradiction because 'data' is a much broader term than 'information', and includes any collection of facts, which can cause conflicts in interpretation with laws that prohibit trading on only 'material non-public information'. The modification causes a significant difference in meaning and enforceability that creates an incompatibility between the provisions based on definition 3, leading to potential legal disputes.
